result.markdown: --- title: "[logrank_test()]{.doc-object-name .doc-function .doc-label .doc-label-function}" body-classes: doc-api-page page-navigation: false html-table-processing: none --- ::: {.doc-subject} Compare survival across groups using the weighted log-rank (G-rho) test. ::: ::: {.doc-usage-source} Usage [Source](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/greenwood/_tests.py#L238-L395){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} ::: ::: {.doc-signature .doc-Kind.FUNCTION} ```python logrank_test(surv, group, *, rho=0.0, gamma=0.0, strata=None) ``` ::: ::: {.doc-text} Tests whether survival curves differ significantly across two or more groups using a chi-square test based on weighted event counts. The test is flexible: with default weights (Fleming-Harrington rho=0, gamma=0), it gives equal weight to all event times (standard log-rank). With rho=1, gamma=0 (Peto-Peto), it emphasizes early events where more subjects are at risk. Other rho/gamma combinations allow custom emphasis on different phases of follow-up. The test compares observed vs. expected event counts under the null hypothesis of equal survival. A large chi-square statistic indicates the groups differ; p-values are interpreted as the probability of seeing such a statistic or larger if survival is truly equal. **Stratification**: Optionally stratify by a nuisance variable (e.g., site, gender) to compute the test within each stratum, then combine results. This controls for confounding while testing group differences. ::: ## Parameters {.doc-parameters} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [surv]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Surv]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : A `Surv` response object representing censored survival times. Supports right-censored data (standard time-to-event) or counting-process format (interval-based data with entry/exit times). Constructed with `Surv.right()`, `Surv.counting()`, or `Surv.multistate()`. [group]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Group labels, one per observation. Can be a Narwhals series (Polars/Pandas), 1-D array, or Python sequence. Labels can be strings, integers, or other hashable types. Must have the same length as `surv`. [rho]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[0.0]{.fl}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Fleming-Harrington weight exponents applied to the pooled Kaplan-Meier survival $S(t-)$ at each event time. The weight is $S(t-)^\rho \, (1-S(t-))^\gamma$. - `rho=0, gamma=0` (default): Standard log-rank test. Equal weight across all times. - `rho=1, gamma=0`: Peto-Peto (Wilcoxon) test. Emphasizes early events. - `rho=0, gamma=1`: Tarone-Ware. Alternative early-event emphasis. - Other (rho, gamma): Flexible emphasis. Higher values emphasize the chosen phase. [gamma]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[0.0]{.fl}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Fleming-Harrington weight exponents applied to the pooled Kaplan-Meier survival $S(t-)$ at each event time. The weight is $S(t-)^\rho \, (1-S(t-))^\gamma$. - `rho=0, gamma=0` (default): Standard log-rank test. Equal weight across all times. - `rho=1, gamma=0`: Peto-Peto (Wilcoxon) test. Emphasizes early events. - `rho=0, gamma=1`: Tarone-Ware. Alternative early-event emphasis. - Other (rho, gamma): Flexible emphasis. Higher values emphasize the chosen phase. [strata]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [None]{.doc-parameter-default} : Optional stratifying factor, one per observation. Same length as `surv`. When provided, the test is computed separately within each stratum, then combined (stratified test). Use to control for confounding or variable that affects baseline hazard but not group differences. Example: stratify by site to account for site-specific differences in survival while testing an overall group effect. ::: ## Returns {.doc-returns} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [TestResult]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : A result object with attributes: - `statistic`: Chi-square test statistic. - `df`: Degrees of freedom (number of groups minus one). - `p_value`: Upper-tail chi-square p-value. Small values indicate survival curves differ. - `method`: Description of the test (e.g., "Log-rank test", "Stratified log-rank test", "G-rho test (rho=1, gamma=0)"). - `observed`: Dictionary mapping group labels to observed weighted event counts. - `expected`: Dictionary mapping group labels to expected event counts under null. ::: ## Details {.doc-details} The log-rank test uses the hypergeometric variance for the chi-square statistic, matching R's `survival::survdiff`. The pooled Kaplan-Meier survivor curve from all groups combined is used to compute the Fleming-Harrington weights, ensuring the test is consistently weighted regardless of group sample sizes. Counting-process data (with entry times) are fully supported, allowing stratification and left-truncation (delayed entry). ## Examples {.doc-examples} Test whether survival differs between the two sexes in the bundled `lung` dataset: ::: {#6c2d071c .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import greenwood as gw lung = gw.load_dataset("lung", backend="polars") y = gw.Surv.right(lung["time"], event=(lung["status"] == 2)) result = gw.logrank_test(y, group=lung["sex"]) result ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ``` TestResult(method='Log-rank test', statistic=10.3267, df=1, p_value=0.001311) ``` ::: ::: Extract individual components from the result: ::: {#fe1af6a2 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} result.statistic # Chi-square statistic ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ``` 10.326741954885637 ``` ::: ::: ::: {#f819d35a .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} result.p_value # P-value for significance ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ``` 0.0013111645203554869 ``` ::: ::: ::: {#15f0b913 .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} result.observed # Observed event counts per group ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ``` {1: 112.0, 2: 53.0} ``` ::: ::: Use the Peto-Peto (Wilcoxon) weighted test to emphasize differences in early survival: ::: {#d63a3296 .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} gw.logrank_test(y, group=lung["sex"], rho=1, gamma=0) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ``` TestResult(method='G-rho test (rho=1, gamma=0)', statistic=12.7142, df=1, p_value=0.0003629) ``` ::: ::: Run a stratified test to control for institution (if available in data): ::: {#42c80949 .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} # gw.logrank_test(y, group=lung["sex"], strata=lung["institution"]) ``` :::
